calendar_tier: TIER 1 — March Madness Final Four weekend. This dominates the issue.

tweet_volume_check.dominant_topic: March Madness — Tier 1 event confirmed by strong tweet volume and calendar. Final Four today leads.

editorial_reasoning.lead_choice: March Madness Final Four — Arizona first Final Four since 2001, Illinois 21-year drought, UConn 35-foot game winner over Duke. Tier 1 event, solid tweet volume, historic droughts ending.

stories_considered_and_demoted.reason: Big money but Tier 3 NFL transaction on Final Four weekend. Around the League item.
Respectful but brief — Around the League honors a veteran without taking space from March Madness.

stories_promoted.reason: NBA investigating franchise — dysfunction angle with BigCat demanding trade tweet is strong content.
Historic rookie record — first teenager to drop 50+ in NBA history. HaterReport stat tweet is perfect.

editorial_reasoning.tweet_gaps: Good reaction content available — HaterReport Dawn Staley coverage, BigCat Giannis take, StatMuse rookie stats all strong.

stories.headline: Arizona and Illinois End Historic Final Four Droughts

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Arizona 25-year drought, Illinois 21-year drought, both #1 seeds vs #2/#3
sub_angles.running_joke: Tommy Lloyd finally gets Arizona there, Brad Underwood's breakthrough
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Duke eliminated by miracle shot, Michigan as other #1 seed
sub_angles.cross_connection: Indianapolis 120 miles from Illinois campus — home crowd advantage
sub_angles.where_it_goes: Championship Monday — can UConn threepeat or will drought end?

research.context: Final Four in Indianapolis, Illinois home court advantage
research.connections: Braylon Mullins 35-footer to beat Duke, historical droughts

blocks.hint: Hook — Arizona in Final Four for first time since 2001, Illinois since 2005. Frame as teams that waited a generation to get back. This is what March Madness does — it resurrects programs.
Illinois gets home court advantage — campus is 120 miles from Indianapolis. Frame as destiny. The Illini faithful can drive to watch their team chase history.

blocks.avoid: Do NOT search basketball — search 'progressive excitement Vince McMahon'

blocks.hint: Kicker — UConn got there with a 35-foot prayer. Braylon Mullins from Greenfield, Indiana — 40 minutes from Lucas Oil Stadium. A local kid sent Duke home. Poetry.

stories.headline: NBA Investigating Giannis-Bucks Medical Drama
hooks: Giannis says he's healthy, team won't clear him

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Failed trade talks in August and February deadline
sub_angles.running_joke: Giannis-Bucks dysfunction, Doc Rivers examining players
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Bucks fans tired of drama, other stars watching

research.context: Player vs team medical clearance dispute
research.connections: BigCat trade demand tweet captures fan sentiment

blocks.hint: Hook — NBA is investigating the Bucks for their handling of Giannis. He says he's healthy, they won't clear him. The league office is now refereeing a medical dispute.
Both sides telling different stories to investigators. Milwaukee says Giannis wants to play but isn't ready. Giannis says he's ready but they won't clear him. Somebody's lying.

blocks.why: Perfect fan sentiment — demand the trade

blocks.avoid: Do NOT search Giannis — search 'this is fine dog fire'

stories.headline: Cooper Flagg Makes History with 51-Point Night
hooks: First teenager in NBA history to score 50+

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: 19 years, 103 days old — younger than any 50-point scorer
sub_angles.who_else_affected: LeBron, Jennings, Booker all older when they did it
sub_angles.cross_connection: College recruit turned NBA instant star

blocks.hint: Cooper Flagg dropped 51 points and became the first teenager in NBA history to score 50+. Not just a rookie record — an age record.

blocks.why: Perfect age breakdown showing he beat LeBron, Jennings, Booker

blocks.hint: Kicker — 19 years, 103 days old. LeBron didn't do it until he was 20. Neither did Jennings or Booker. Kid's already rewriting history.

stories.headline: Luka Dončić Done for Season with Hamstring Injury

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Led NBA in scoring, missed time for child's birth
sub_angles.cross_connection: Birth in Slovenia, back two days later shows dedication
sub_angles.where_it_goes: MVP vote controversy, Lakers playoff push without star

research.context: Agent fighting for MVP eligibility despite injury

blocks.hint: His agent is applying for an exception to the 65-game rule. The argument: Luka missed two games for his daughter's birth in Slovenia, was back competing two days later. Extraordinary circumstances.
Kicker — 33.5 points per game, leading the league. Now voters have to decide: does the best scorer deserve MVP even if his season's over?

stories.headline: Dawn Staley vs Geno Auriemma Final Four Drama
hooks: Geno claimed she didn't shake hands pregame

sub_angles.running_joke: Geno being petty in defeat vs gracious Dawn
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Women's basketball getting attention for drama
sub_angles.cross_connection: Men's and women's Final Four same weekend

blocks.hint: Geno Auriemma accused Dawn Staley of not shaking his hand before the game. Problem: there's video of her shaking his hand. He lied for no reason.

blocks.why: Contrast showing how different Geno acts when he wins vs loses

stories.headline: Trump Signs College Sports Executive Order
hooks: 5 years to play, one transfer rule

blocks.hint: 74-page executive order. Five years to play five seasons, one free transfer. Takes effect August 1 but will face legal challenges on multiple fronts. Play it straight — politics meets sports policy.

timeline.why: Bracket of Randomness Elite Eight matchup
Rashee Rice cleared by NFL investigation

closer.hook: Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld baby announcement

blocks.hint: Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld announced the birth of their first child. Bills Mafia just got its newest member.
